reasoning: The ORIGINAL and CORRECTED formalizations have identical theorem statements. The only differences are non-semantic: the CORRECTED adds `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000` (a compilation resource setting) and `open Filter Topology Set` (namespace imports). Both formalizations encode: f has continuous third derivative (ContDiff ℝ 3 f), f^(n)(x) > 0 for all n ≤ 3 and all x (covering f, f', f'', f''' positivity), f'''(x) ≤ f(x), and conclude f'(x) < 2f(x). This is a faithful encoding of the Putnam 1999 B4 problem. Since `iteratedDeriv 0 f = f`, `iteratedDeriv 1 f = deriv f`, etc., the use of `iteratedDeriv n f` with `n ≤ 3` correctly captures all four positivity conditions. The ORIGINAL is logically equivalent to the CORRECTED and faithful to the informal statement.
